name: Ascend Scheduler
description: Cron expression parsing, next-run computation, and macOS launchd plist generation for scheduled job management. Converts human-friendly schedule specifications to cron expressions, computes next run times, and manages launchd plists for periodic task execution.

types.name: _DAY_MAP
types.description: Mapping from day names (full and abbreviated) to cron weekday numbers (0=Sunday)

types.name: _CRON_WEEKDAY_TO_PYTHON
types.description: Mapping from cron weekday numbers (0=Sunday) to Python weekday numbers (0=Monday, 6=Sunday)

types.name: CronFields

fields.name: dom
fields.description: Day of month field (1-31, *, ranges, lists)

fields.name: dow
fields.description: Day of week field (0-6, *, ranges, lists, 0=Sunday)

types.description: Dictionary representing parsed cron expression fields

functions.name: schedule_to_cron
functions.description: Convert human-friendly schedule specification to a standard 5-field cron expression (minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week).

inputs.name: biweekly
inputs.description: Day name for biweekly execution (2nd and 4th week)

inputs.name: monthly
inputs.description: Comma-separated day numbers for monthly execution

error_cases.name: UnknownDayError
error_cases.condition: weekly or biweekly day name not in _DAY_MAP (case-insensitive)

error_cases.name: NoScheduleError
error_cases.condition: No schedule frequency parameter is specified

preconditions: At least one schedule frequency parameter (daily, weekdays, weekly, biweekly, monthly, quarterly) must be truthy
If weekly or biweekly is provided, the day name must exist in _DAY_MAP
postconditions: Returns a valid 5-field cron expression string
Cron format is: 'minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week'

functions.name: parse_cron
functions.description: Parse a 5-field cron expression into a dictionary with named components.

inputs.name: expr
inputs.description: Cron expression string with 5 space-separated fields

error_cases.name: InvalidCronFieldCount
error_cases.condition: expr does not contain exactly 5 fields after splitting by whitespace

preconditions: expr must contain exactly 5 whitespace-separated fields
postconditions: Returns dict with keys: minute, hour, dom, month, dow
Each value is the original string field (may contain *, ranges, lists)

functions.name: _expand_field
functions.description: Expand a cron field (*, range, list) into a set of integer values within the given bounds.

inputs.name: min_val
inputs.description: Minimum valid value for this field

inputs.name: max_val
inputs.description: Maximum valid value for this field

error_cases.name: InvalidFieldFormat
error_cases.condition: field contains non-numeric characters outside of valid syntax

preconditions: field contains only *, digits, commas, and hyphens
Range syntax uses single hyphen between two integers
postconditions: Returns set of all matching integer values in [min_val, max_val]
'*' expands to all values in range
Comma-separated values are unioned
Range 'a-b' expands to all integers from a to b inclusive

functions.name: compute_next_run
functions.description: Compute the next scheduled run time from a cron expression, searching up to 366 days ahead. Uses cron OR-semantics when both day-of-month and day-of-week are restricted.

inputs.name: after
inputs.description: Reference datetime to compute next run after (defaults to now)

error_cases.name: InvalidCronExpression
error_cases.condition: cron_expr cannot be parsed by parse_cron

preconditions: cron_expr is a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Returns ISO format datetime string 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM' if a match is found within 366 days
Returns empty string '' if no match found within 366 days
Returned time is strictly after the 'after' parameter
Seconds and microseconds are zeroed out

structured_side_effects.description: Reads current time if after is None

functions.name: describe_cron
functions.description: Convert a cron expression to a human-readable English description.

error_cases.name: InvalidCronExpression
error_cases.condition: cron_expr cannot be parsed by parse_cron

preconditions: cron_expr is a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Returns human-readable schedule description
Falls back to returning the original cron_expr if no pattern matches

functions.name: _find_ascend_path
functions.description: Locate the ascend executable by searching PATH and common installation locations.
postconditions: Returns path to ascend executable if found via shutil.which or common locations
Returns 'ascend' as fallback if not found (relies on PATH at runtime)

functions.name: _cron_to_calendar_intervals
functions.description: Convert a cron expression to launchd StartCalendarInterval XML format.
preconditions: cron_expr is a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Returns XML string representing launchd CalendarInterval dict or array
Single interval: returns <dict>...</dict>
Multiple intervals: returns <array><dict>...</dict>...</array>
Simplifies complex schedules by selecting min values for minute/hour if <= 2 values, else defaults to 0 or 9

functions.name: generate_plist
functions.description: Generate metadata for a launchd plist (label, ascend path, log path). Returns a 3-tuple instead of plist content.
postconditions: Returns tuple of (label, ascend_path, log_path)
label format: 'com.ascend.schedule.{schedule_name}'
log_path format: '{schedules_dir}/{schedule_name}.log'

functions.name: write_plist
functions.description: Generate and write a launchd plist file to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ for the given schedule.

error_cases.name: InvalidCronExpression
error_cases.condition: cron_expr cannot be converted to calendar intervals

preconditions: cron_expr is a valid 5-field cron expression
postconditions: Creates ~/Library/LaunchAgents directory if it doesn't exist
Writes plist file to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.ascend.schedule.{schedule_name}.plist
Returns Path to written plist file
Plist contains zsh invocation: '/bin/zsh -l -c {ascend_path} schedule-run {schedule_name}'

structured_side_effects.description: Creates LaunchAgents directory and writes plist file

functions.name: load_plist
functions.description: Load a launchd plist using launchctl. Returns True on success, False on failure.
postconditions: Returns False if plist file doesn't exist
Returns True if launchctl load succeeds
Returns False if launchctl load fails or times out

structured_side_effects.description: Executes 'launchctl load' subprocess with 10 second timeout

functions.name: unload_plist
functions.description: Unload a launchd plist using launchctl. Returns True on success, False on failure.
postconditions: Returns False if plist file doesn't exist
Returns True if launchctl unload succeeds
Returns False if launchctl unload fails or times out

structured_side_effects.description: Executes 'launchctl unload' subprocess with 10 second timeout

functions.name: remove_plist
functions.description: Unload and delete a launchd plist file. Returns True if file was deleted, False otherwise.
postconditions: Calls unload_plist first to unload the job
Returns True if plist file existed and was deleted
Returns False if plist file doesn't exist

structured_side_effects.description: Deletes plist file if it exists

invariants: Cron weekday 0 = Sunday (differs from Python's datetime.weekday() where 0 = Monday)
Cron expressions are always 5 fields: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week
When both day-of-month and day-of-week are restricted (not *), cron uses OR semantics (match either)
compute_next_run searches up to 366 days ahead before giving up
launchd plist files are stored in ~/Library/LaunchAgents/
_PLIST_TEMPLATE defines XML structure for macOS launchd configuration
Biweekly schedules target 2nd and 4th occurrences (days 8-14 and 22-28)
Quarterly schedules run on 1st day of Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct

data_access.rationale: Reads from filesystem to locate ascend executable and check plist existence. Writes plist files to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/. Reads system clock for current time in compute_next_run.

side_effects.rationale: Executes launchctl load/unload commands to manage launchd jobs
Creates and deletes launchd plist configuration files

authority.rationale: This module is the authoritative source for schedule parsing, cron expression handling, and launchd plist management within the Ascend system
